description:

Intraocular Pressure: OD 13 mmHg / OI 12 mmHg — both within normal range (<21 mmHg).

Fundus Examination (undilated): Optic discs and maculae normal; posterior pole without alterations in both eyes.

No follow-up recommendations specified.

Plan: Once results received and if normal, continue annual urological check-ups.

Pulmonary function test (Spirometry/Plethysmography) performed 12/03/2025 at Sanitas, Dept. of Pneumology
Patient: Male, 41 years, 180 cm, 76 kg

Key spirometric results (pre-bronchodilator):
- FVC: 4.93 L (89% of predicted) — normal
- FEV1: 3.86 L (88% of predicted) — normal
- FEV1/FVC ratio: 78.26% (101% of predicted) — normal, no obstruction
- MEF50: 3.85 L/s (73% of predicted) — mildly reduced mid-expiratory flow
- PEF: 8.43 L/s (81% of predicted) — normal
- MEF25: 1.65 L/s (100% of predicted) — normal

No post-bronchodilator test performed.

Technical note: Suboptimal technique — forced expiration time was less than 6 seconds, which may slightly underestimate FVC.

Conclusion (by Dr. María Isabel Badorrey Martín): Spirometric values within normal range. No obstructive or restrictive pattern identified.

aiName: Stress Test (Treadmill Bruce Protocol) - March 2025 - Hospital Sanitas Cima Barcelona
description:
Stress test (ergometry) performed on 12/03/2025 at Hospital Sanitas Cima, Barcelona. Reason: routine cardiac check-up.

Key results — all within normal limits:
- Protocol: Bruce treadmill, total duration 7:36 min
- Max workload achieved: 17.30 METs — excellent functional capacity for age and sex (41-year-old male)
- Max heart rate reached: 176 bpm = 98% of age-predicted maximum (target ≥85%) — appropriate response
- Resting blood pressure: 110/55 mmHg; max BP during exercise: 160/65 mmHg — normal response
- No chest pain (angina) during test
- No arrhythmias detected
- No ST-segment changes (no signs of myocardial ischemia)
- Test stopped due to muscular fatigue (not cardiac limitation)

Overall conclusion: Stress test clinically and electrocardiographically NEGATIVE for myocardial ischemia. Good functional capacity. No cardiac concerns identified.

description:

Medical background noted: Dyslipidemia (elevated blood lipids — managed separately). No allergies, no toxic habits.

Study: Carotid and vertebral artery Doppler ultrasound (Eco Doppler TSA), performed 12/03/2025 at Hospital Sanitas Cima, Barcelona.

Right side: Common, internal, external carotid and vertebral arteries — normal appearance, patent, normal spectral waveforms. ICA/CCA index <2. No atherosclerotic plaques causing significant stenosis. Vertebral artery patent with antegrade flow. Intimal thickness: 0.4 mm (normal).

Left side: Common, internal, external carotid and vertebral arteries — normal appearance, patent, normal spectral waveforms. ICA/CCA index <2. No atherosclerotic plaques causing significant stenosis. Vertebral artery patent with antegrade flow. Intimal thickness: 0.5 mm (normal, ref: <0.9 mm).

Subclavian arteries: Patent bilaterally with normal triphasic flow.

Conclusion: Completely normal study — no significant vascular abnormalities detected. No signs of atherosclerosis or stenosis in the neck vessels supplying the brain.

aiName: Sanitas CIMA Health Checkup Report - March 2025
description:

Sigmoid colon polyp (5mm) — detected at 40cm from anal margin, removed during colonoscopy (polypectomy performed); histopathology (anatomía patológica) result PENDING — follow-up colonoscopy frequency will depend on pathology result

Await sigmoid polyp pathology result — determines colonoscopy surveillance interval

Annual general checkup

aiName: Blood Panel Checkup Results 2025-03-12 Sanitas CIMA Barcelona
description:
RDW (anisocytosis index) 11.3% — below reference range (12.2–16.4%), low RDW is generally not clinically concerning and may indicate uniform red cell size
% Monocytes 13.0% — mildly elevated (ref: 5.3–12.4%); absolute monocyte count 0.6 10e3/µL is within normal range; mild monocytosis can be associated with minor infection or inflammation, typically not alarming in isolation
Bilirubin total 1.13 mg/dL — marginally above upper reference (ref: 0.3–1.2); slight elevation, may suggest mild Gilbert's syndrome or transient cause; liver enzymes (GOT, GPT, GGT) all normal
Hepatitis B surface antibody (anti-HBs) <2.0 mIU/mL — below protective threshold of 10 mIU/mL; patient has NO immunity against Hepatitis B (no prior infection, no effective vaccination); Hepatitis B surface antigen and core antibody both negative; Hepatitis C antibody negative. Hepatitis B vaccination is recommended.
Lipid profile: all within normal range — Total cholesterol 167 mg/dL (<200), LDL 101 mg/dL (<130), HDL 50 mg/dL (>40), Triglycerides 80 mg/dL (<150); cardiovascular risk indices favorable
Thyroid function normal: TSH 2.05 mcU/mL, T3 0.90 ng/mL, Free T4 1.07 ng/dL — all within reference
Tumor markers (CEA 1.42, AFP 1.51) within normal limits
Glucose 93 mg/dL, kidney function (creatinine 0.89, eGFR >90), coagulation, and urinalysis all normal

description:

• 250 Hz: 10 dB, 500 Hz: 10 dB, 1000 Hz: 5 dB, 2000 Hz: 0 dB, 4000 Hz: 15 dB, 8000 Hz: 10 dB
• Hearing loss (AMA scale): 0.5% — within normal limits

• Hearing loss (AMA scale): 0.6% — within normal limits

All thresholds are within the normal hearing range (≤25 dB across frequencies).
Conclusion: 'Dentro de la normalidad' — results entirely normal bilaterally. No significant hearing loss detected.

Overall conclusion: No significant abnormalities — normal echocardiogram.
Left ventricle (LV): Normal size and wall thickness, excellent systolic function. Ejection fraction (EF): 70% (normal ≥55%).
Right heart: Normal dimensions and function. TAPSE 23 mm (normal ≥17 mm).
Left atrium & aortic root: Not dilated (LA 34 mm, aortic root 30 mm — both within normal limits).
Mitral valve: Morphologically normal; minimal regurgitation (Grade I — clinically insignificant). Normal diastolic filling pattern (E/A ratio 1.34).
Aortic valve: Appears tricuspid with thin leaflets, normal opening/closing; no regurgitation. Max velocity 1.08 m/s, max gradient 4.7 mmHg — normal.
Tricuspid valve: No regurgitation detected.
Pericardium: No pericardial effusion.
Inferior vena cava: Not dilated, good inspiratory collapse — normal right atrial pressure.

aiName: Colonoscopy Report - March 2025 - Sigmoid Polyp Removed
description:
Procedure: Colonoscopy (colorectal cancer screening) with polypectomy, performed 13/03/2025 at Hospital Sanitas Cima, Barcelona.
Findings: Small 5mm polyp found in the sigmoid colon at 40cm from the anal margin — successfully removed with forceps (polypectomy).
Other findings: Grade I-II internal hemorrhoids (uncomplicated); rectal exam normal; cecal floor, ileocecal valve, and appendicular foramen all normal; mucosa and vascularization otherwise normal throughout.
Bowel preparation quality: Good (Boston score 2-2-2).
No immediate complications reported.
Important: Seek emergency care if severe abdominal pain or bleeding occurs after the procedure.
Pathology result of removed polyp not included — follow-up may be needed to confirm histology and determine next colonoscopy interval.

KEY FINDING – Left kidney: 13mm partially calcified nodular lesion, classified as Bosniak IIF cyst — requires follow-up imaging (CT or MRI) to monitor for potential malignant transformation
Left kidney size: 12.8 x 5.1 cm (normal); right kidney: 11 x 4.4 cm (normal); no stones, no hydronephrosis in either kidney
Prostate: 22cc volume (normal for age), inhomogeneous echotexture with surgical capsule fibrosis noted — clinically relevant finding worth discussing with urologist
Bladder: pre-void volume 421cc, smooth walls, no masses; post-void residual urine: zero (normal)
Abdominal ultrasound: liver, pancreas, spleen all normal; no focal liver lesions; gallbladder distended with thin walls and no gallstones
Portal system and abdominal aorta: normal caliber and flow
Action required: Bosniak IIF cysts carry a ~5-10% risk of malignancy and require periodic imaging surveillance — follow-up should be arranged with a urologist
